close_reason: k8s uses 'key' because Kubernetes Secrets have a 'data' map with 'keys'. This is domain-correct naming for k8s. Renaming to 'field' would be confusing in the k8s context. The inconsistency is intentional and matches each backend's domain terminology.
description: The concept of 'select one value from a multi-value secret container' uses two different URI parameter names: k8s uses ?key=<data-key> to select a key from the Kubernetes Secret's .data map. aws-sm, azure-kv, gcp-sm (not supported), hashicorp-vault, and the ?field= rejection messages in bitwarden/doppler/keyring/wolfhsm all use ?field=. A caller building a URI abstraction layer must special-case k8s. A maintainer adding a new backend must choose between the two names with no documented rationale. Fix: rename k8s's ?key= to ?field= for consistency with the rest of the workspace, OR document in secretx-core which concept maps to which name and why k8s is different.
title: Sub-value selector param named 'key' in k8s but 'field' in all other backends
